---
title: OrgTree Policy Targeting — Intune + Azure Policy Dual Transport (UIAO_164) — Adapter
  Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: entra-policy-targeting
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# OrgTree Policy Targeting — Intune + Azure Policy Dual Transport (UIAO_164) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The OrgTree Policy Targeting Adapter is the **policy-binding actuator** of the UIAO OrgTree substrate. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it binds existing Intune configuration profiles and existing Azure Policy definitions to the OrgTree — making sure the right policies enforce against the right organizational populations, without taking ownership of the policy contents themselves.

The split between *policy authoring* and *policy targeting* is intentional. UIAO does not author Intune settings catalogs or Azure Policy rule expressions; existing tooling (and existing change-control authority) does that. What UIAO governs is the *binding* — which population a given profile applies to, and which subscription scope or resource group a given Azure Policy assignment covers. That binding is canon-governed; the policy body is not.

Two primary functions:

1. **Intune profile assignment.** For each Intune configuration profile referenced in UIAO_164, computes the OrgTree-* dynamic group ([`entra-dynamic-groups`](../entra-dynamic-groups/entra-dynamic-groups.html)) that represents the intended population and writes the assignment via Microsoft Graph `/deviceManagement/configurationPolicies/{id}/assignments`. Membership flows from the group — when an OrgPath assignment changes upstream, Intune compliance evaluation picks up the new device set on its next sync without this adapter needing to re-run.
2. **Azure Policy assignment scoping.** For each Azure Policy definition referenced in UIAO_164, writes a policy assignment scoped to the appropriate management group, subscription, or resource group, with the policy parameter `orgPathPrefix` populated from canon and `matchMode` selecting the prefix-match operator. Arc-enrolled machines are filtered by their `OrgPath` ARM tag (written by [`entra-device-orgpath`](../entra-device-orgpath/entra-device-orgpath.html)), so the assignment evaluates only the intended population.

Policy-consumer drift — phantom assignments (no canon row), missing Intune profiles, missing Azure Policy definitions, or definitions that do not accept the canonical `orgPathPrefix` / `matchMode` parameters — is **never** auto-remediated. The blast radius of automated policy changes is the broadest in the stack, so corrections route through the UIAO_155 governance workflows before any apply lands.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/entra_policy_targeting.py`. `plan()` and `apply(dry_run=True)` are offline-safe; `apply(dry_run=False)` issues Graph and ARM mutations limited to the assignment/binding surface.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `intune-policy-assignments`, `azure-policy-arc-assignments`, `orgtree-policy-targeting`

**Reads:** UIAO_164 policy-targeting table (executable canon at `canon/data/orgpath/policy-targets.yaml`), UIAO_151 codebook (every Arc prefix must resolve to an active code), UIAO_152 dynamic-group canon (every Intune target group must resolve to a UIAO_152 group), UIAO_153 device-plane canon. Live state reads via Microsoft Graph (Intune assignments) and Azure Resource Manager (Azure Policy assignments).

**Emits:** `policy-targeting-plan.json` (planned assignment changes with per-row provenance back to UIAO_164), `policy-targeting-report.json` (post-apply audit trail), and skipped-manual findings for phantom assignments and contract-violating policy definitions.

**Does NOT:** author or modify Intune profile contents, author or modify Azure Policy definition contents, manage Azure Policy initiative (policy-set) authoring, touch Conditional Access policy bodies (separate workflow), or auto-remediate phantom assignments. Policy *bodies* are explicitly out of scope — this adapter binds, it does not author. Apply paths are gated on `dry_run=False`.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CM-2`, `CM-6`, `CM-7`, `CM-8`, `AC-3`, `CA-7`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CM-2** Baseline Configuration | Primary | The canonical UIAO_164 table is the baseline record of which policies apply to which OrgPath populations; assignment plans surface deviation from that baseline. |
| **CM-6** Configuration Settings | Primary | Intune and Azure Policy assignments are the enforcement edge for configuration settings; this adapter ensures those edges target the correct OrgPath population. |
| **CM-7** Least Functionality | Supporting | Scoping policy assignments to OrgPath populations is how least-functionality is operationalized — controls apply only to the populations that warrant them. |
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Supporting | Plan and report artifacts enumerate every assignment, the UIAO_164 row that authorized it, and the policy definition / Intune profile that it binds. |
| **AC-3** Access Enforcement | Primary | Policy assignments are the access-enforcement edge for both device configuration (Intune) and server configuration (Azure Policy / Guest Configuration). |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Supporting | Post-apply reports and drift findings provide continuous evidence that the assignment surface remains aligned with canon. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Binds to UIAO_164 through ADR-039; executable canon at `canon/data/orgpath/policy-targets.yaml`, JSON Schema at `schemas/orgpath/policy-targets.schema.json`. Depends on UIAO_151 (codebook, ADR-035), UIAO_152 (dynamic groups, ADR-036), and UIAO_153 (device planes, ADR-038) — every Intune target group must resolve to a UIAO_152 group; every Arc prefix must resolve to an active UIAO_151 code.

Azure Policy definitions referenced from UIAO_164 **must** accept the canonical parameters `orgPathPrefix` and `matchMode`. Definitions that do not honor this parameter contract surface as drift; policy authors follow the contract or the assignment is skipped-manual.

Policy *bodies* are out of scope — only the targeting / scope binding is canon-governed. Policy-consumer drift (phantom assignments, missing profiles, missing definitions) is never auto-remediated — blast radius is the broadest in the stack.

ZTMM pillars: identity, applications-and-workloads.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- UIAO_007 — OrgTree Modernization: AD to Entra ID
- UIAO_151 — OrgPath Codebook
- UIAO_152 — Dynamic Group Library
- UIAO_153 — Attribute Mapping Table
- UIAO_164 — Policy Targeting Library

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
